summary: E2E tests never touch a real account — every authenticating test creates an ephemeral user for that run and deletes it at the end, without exception.

rules.rule: No test may perform a destructive auth action on a real account or reuse a real account as the subject of a test, even read-only, under any circumstance.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Auth tests create an ephemeral user in setup with the canonical format e2e.<test-slug>.<unix-timestamp>@<test-domain> and delete the whole account in teardown — leaving ephemeral users uncleaned is prohibited.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Do not hardcode real-account emails in test files (**/*.spec.*, **/*.test.*, e2e/**, playwright/**, cypress/** and equivalents), and do not use a service-role/admin credential on the auth store except to create and clean up the test's own ephemeral users.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Tests needing an elevated role create an ephemeral E2E user with the role assigned at creation time and delete the whole account in teardown — never a team account, not even a test-tenant admin.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Wire a pre-commit/CI governance check that blocks test-file emails on product/client domains lacking the e2e. prefix and destructive auth-admin calls without a matching createUser on the same id, plus a post-run residue check that reports e2e. users older than 24h.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: On discovering a test touched a real account: STOP running tests and deploying, document the incident as a FINDING-E2E-USER-CONTAMINATION-<date> with evidence, revert auth-store changes using values authorized by the human authority, and open a hotfix removing the violation.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
